heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Built for Liverpool
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Liverpool | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Liverpool homes, from Victorian terraces to suburban semis. Built in Blackburn in weeks, installed in days.
intro:
Liverpool's homes have real character — the red-brick Victorian terraces of Wavertree and Anfield, the generous semis of Childwall and Woolton, the coastal streets of Crosby. What many of them share is a familiar frustration: a kitchen that's too tight, a dining space that doubles as everything else, and nowhere quiet to work. Moving isn't the only answer.

EazyBase designs and builds modular home extensions in Liverpool a different way. Your extension is precision-built at our factory in Blackburn — often in as little as four weeks — then delivered and installed at your home in under a week. That means weeks of disruption, not months, and a finish that's been quality-checked indoors rather than assembled in the rain.

Whether it's a bigger kitchen, a light-filled dining room, a home office or a playroom for the kids, we handle design, build and installation as one seamless process — so you can get on with life while your extension takes shape forty miles up the road.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Liverpool terrace
localAngles.body: Victorian terraces in areas like Wavertree, Anfield and Kensington were built long before open-plan living, and the narrow side return and modest rear yard are often the only places to grow. A well-designed rear or side-return extension can transform a galley kitchen into a proper family space without losing the character of the house. Because our modules are engineered to precise dimensions in the factory, they suit the tight, repetitive footprints of terraced streets — we design around your party walls, your yard and your light, then install quickly with minimal upheaval for you and your neighbours.

localAngles.heading: More room for semis in Childwall, Woolton and Crosby
localAngles.body: The interwar and post-war semis of Childwall, Woolton, Allerton and Crosby usually offer something terraces don't: a decent garden and space at the side or rear. That opens up more options — a full-width rear extension for a kitchen-diner, a garden room for working from home, or a playroom that keeps the toys out of the lounge. Factory building means the design is fixed and costed before anything arrives on your drive, so the garden isn't a building site all summer and the finished room feels like it was always part of the house.

localAngles.heading: Planning and conservation areas in Liverpool
localAngles.body: Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, which means many Liverpool projects don't need a full planning application — but always check with Liverpool City Council first (or Sefton Council if you're in Crosby). It matters most in the city's conservation areas, such as those around Sefton Park and the Georgian Quarter, where extra rules can apply to materials, rooflines and visibility from the street. We design with these constraints in mind from day one and can guide you through the checks, so there are no surprises after you've fallen in love with a design.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works so well here
localAngles.body: Traditional extensions in Liverpool mean months of skips, scaffolding and trades squeezing down terraced streets — and plenty of weather delays. We build your extension indoors at our Blackburn factory, under controlled conditions, in as little as four weeks. It then travels down the M58 or M62 and is installed at your home in under a week. Shorter time on site means less disturbance for close-packed neighbours, less risk to your garden, and a build quality that doesn't depend on what the Mersey weather decided to do that month.

faqs.q: How long does a modular extension take for a home in Liverpool?
faqs.a: Your extension is built at our factory in Blackburn — around an hour from Liverpool — often in as little as four weeks. On-site installation at your home then typically takes under a week. Compared with a traditional build, you spend far less time living alongside the works, and the overall programme is agreed with you before we start.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Liverpool?
faqs.a: Not always. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions within certain size limits, though you should always confirm with Liverpool City Council (or Sefton Council for Crosby addresses) before starting. If your home sits in a conservation area — for example near Sefton Park or the Georgian Quarter — additional rules may apply. We design with planning in mind and can help you understand what your project needs.

faqs.q: Can you install on a tight terraced street with on-street parking?
faqs.a: Yes — it's one of the things we plan for. During your site survey we assess access, parking and delivery routes, whether that's a narrow street in Wavertree or a cul-de-sac in Childwall. Modules are sized and sequenced to suit your plot, and because installation takes days rather than months, any disruption to parking and neighbours is short-lived.

faqs.q: What do Liverpool homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: The most popular projects are open-plan kitchens and kitchen-diners, followed by home offices, dining rooms, kids' playrooms and garden rooms. In terraces, side-return and rear extensions usually unlock the kitchen space; in semis with bigger gardens, a garden room or full-width rear extension is often the better fit. We'll help you choose the right approach for your house and how you live.
